globalFindings.description: Auth strategy: httpOnly session cookies instead of bearer tokens. FE schema included a 'token' field in AdminLoginResponse — removed in favor of server-managed session cookie for security.
globalFindings.impact: FE must read user data from response body, not decode a JWT. Session validation via GET /api/auth/me.
globalFindings.resolution: Applied in auth_accounts openapi.json. FE action: remove token handling code, use cookie-based session.

globalFindings.description: Added POST /api/auth/logout endpoint — required by AA-REQ-02 ('Admin can log out from any page') but absent from FE endpoint proposals.
globalFindings.impact: FE needs to call this on logout button click.

globalFindings.description: Added GET /api/auth/me endpoint — required for FE to validate session on app load and enforce GP-05 (unauthenticated admin access redirects to login).
globalFindings.impact: FE should call this on app initialization to check auth state.

globalFindings.description: Added GET /api/navigation/menu endpoint — returns role-based menu items per global contract role visibility rules. Keeps navigation config server-driven.
globalFindings.impact: FE sidebar can render dynamically from this API instead of hardcoding visibility rules.

globalFindings.description: Consistent error envelope across all modules: { error: string, message: string, statusCode: number }. Maps to FE ApiError Zod schema.
globalFindings.impact: FE can use a single error handler for all API responses.

globalFindings.description: Pagination convention: offset-based with page/limit query params. Response always includes total/page/limit fields.
globalFindings.impact: Consistent across lead_management, program_pricing, payment_tracking, communication log.

globalFindings.description: English-only in MVP. No localization, no locale headers, no multi-language response fields.
globalFindings.impact: If localization is needed later, endpoints serving user-visible text will need locale handling added.

globalFindings.description: Added 429 (rate limit) responses on login and forgot-password endpoints to prevent brute-force attacks.
globalFindings.impact: FE should handle 429 by showing 'Too many attempts, try again later' message.

globalFindings.description: Added 410 (Gone) response on reset-password for expired tokens, distinct from 400 (bad request).
globalFindings.impact: FE can show different messaging for expired vs invalid tokens.

globalFindings.description: Added 409 (Conflict) responses for duplicate scenarios: coupon code already exists, child already enrolled in program, trial already booked.
globalFindings.impact: FE can show specific error messages for these cases.

globalFindings.description: POST endpoints that create resources return 201 (Created) instead of 200 (OK) for RESTful consistency.
globalFindings.impact: FE should treat both 200 and 201 as success.

globalFindings.description: Added admin enrollment management endpoints: GET /api/enrollments (list with filters), GET /api/enrollments/{enrollmentId} (detail), PATCH /api/enrollments/{enrollmentId}/status (cancel/complete). Resolves BLK-01.
globalFindings.impact: FE needs an admin enrollment list screen at /admin/enrollments and detail screen at /admin/enrollments/:enrollmentId. These were implied by global nav but had no backing endpoints.

globalFindings.description: ET-A-02: Instant enrollment confirmed for MVP. No approval workflow. Admin cancels after the fact if needed. Resolves BLK-02.
globalFindings.impact: submitEnrollment returns confirmed enrollment immediately. Admin uses PATCH /api/enrollments/{enrollmentId}/status to cancel if needed.

findings.description: FE AdminLoginResponse schema includes 'token' field. BE uses httpOnly cookie instead. FE should remove token field from its Zod schema and response handling.
No POST /api/leads endpoint exists for manual lead creation. Lead sources include 'manual' but FE doesn't propose a create-lead screen or endpoint. Admin may need this for phone/walk-in leads.
No webhook/ingest endpoint for Facebook Lead Forms or Google Forms. These external lead sources need a BE-only ingestion mechanism (not FE-facing). Deferred per technical_parking_lot.
LM-A-01 (returning parents identified by email match) is pending_human_review. BE contract proceeds with email-based matching but this could change.
PP-A-02 (capacity is per-program, not per-session) is pending_human_review. Schema uses a single 'capacity' field at program level.

RESOLVED: Added admin enrollment management endpoints: GET /api/enrollments (list), GET /api/enrollments/{enrollmentId} (detail), PATCH /api/enrollments/{enrollmentId}/status (cancel/complete). Global nav 'Enrollments' section is now backed by API endpoints.

RESOLVED: User confirmed instant enrollment for MVP (Option A). No approval flow. Admin can cancel enrollments after the fact via PATCH /api/enrollments/{enrollmentId}/status. Assumption ET-A-02 is now confirmed.
ET-A-04 (no waitlist in MVP) means program-full returns 400/409. If waitlist is added later, a new endpoint would be needed.

Payment overdue threshold check is a background job concern (not an API endpoint). HO-04 handoff (payment_tracking -> communication) fires when overdue threshold is exceeded. This is BE-internal logic, not surfaced as an FE-callable endpoint.

No DELETE endpoint for templates. Default templates are not deletable (CM-A-01). Custom templates also have no delete path in FE proposals. If delete is needed for custom templates, FE must add the interaction.

global.description: All enums classified as stable after volatility check. No lookup tables needed for MVP. Lead sources, statuses, payment statuses, etc. are all engineering-controlled values that require code changes to modify.
global.impact: Enum migrations needed if values are added, but all additions require corresponding code changes anyway.

global.description: Admin.role uses String type (not enum) because only 'admin' exists in MVP. Staff and parent roles are Phase 2. Avoids single-value enum.
global.impact: Role-based access enforced at application level. Migration to enum when Phase 2 roles are added.

global.description: Denormalized parent info (parentName, parentEmail, parentPhone) stored on both Trial and Enrollment models. Lead is authoritative source but denormalization supports public form flows without lead lookup.
global.impact: Parent info updates on Lead do not automatically propagate to Trial/Enrollment records. Acceptable for MVP since these are point-in-time snapshots.

global.description: Computed fields (enrollmentCount, totalFee, spotsRemaining, remainingBalance) are NOT stored in DB. Calculated at query time from source data.
global.impact: No stale data risk. Dashboard and list queries use JOINs/COUNTs instead of cached values.

global.description: CommunicationLog has no FK to Template or Automation. Stores snapshot data (templateName, interpolated subject) for audit independence. Template changes don't affect historical log entries.
global.impact: Log entries are immutable audit records. Retry creates new entries.

global.description: English-only MVP (GF-07). No localization storage strategy needed. Localization checklist not applicable for current scope.
global.impact: If localization is added later, localized content fields will need migration.

findings.description: A-01/LM-A-01: Lead email uniqueness (returning parents identified by email match) is pending_human_review. DB contract proceeds with unique constraint on Lead.email as default.
A-12/PP-A-02: Capacity is per-program (not per-session) is pending_human_review. DB contract uses single capacity field at program level. Isolated for future per-session migration.

GP-01 (one enrollment per child per program) cannot be enforced as a DB unique constraint because child identity is based on name+age (not a unique identifier). Enforced at application level by checking (childName, childAge, programId) before insert.
PT-A-01/A-07: Payment overdue threshold (default 3 days) is admin-configurable but no dedicated config field exists in LocationSettings. Application config or environment variable assumed for MVP.
CM-A-01: 6 default templates and 6 automation rows require seed data on first deployment. No DB-level protection against deleting defaults — isDefault flag checked at application level.

resolvedBlockers.description: No admin enrollment management screen or endpoints.
resolvedBlockers.resolution: Added 3 admin enrollment endpoints to enrollment_trial module: GET /api/enrollments, GET /api/enrollments/{enrollmentId}, PATCH /api/enrollments/{enrollmentId}/status. Supports list, detail, cancel, and complete actions.

resolvedBlockers.description: ET-A-02 (instant enrollment without approval) was pending human review.
resolvedBlockers.resolution: User confirmed instant enrollment for MVP. No approval flow needed. Admin can cancel after the fact using the new enrollment status endpoint. Assumption ET-A-02 upgraded from pending_human_review to user_approved.

crossModuleHandoffValidation.notes: Admin sends trial link via lead management -> parent lands on trial booking form via public URL
Enrollment creates payment record. Confirmation page shows Zelle instructions. Separate payment instructions page also available.
Background email fires via automation engine. No dedicated API call from FE — handled BE-side.

endpoints: BE background job checks payment overdue threshold -> triggers payment_overdue automation
crossModuleHandoffValidation.notes: No FE-facing endpoint. Handled by BE scheduler/cron job.
Parent selects trial date from calendar -> navigates to trial booking form with date pre-selected
